meaning: n.看守,守卫,看门人,典狱官
examples: A few days later, we were having our lunch under the shed when this warder wandered over.
However, when the same warder looked in his cell through an observation window just over an hour later, she saw him hanging.
But for Mr Steinberg's tough prison-warder colleagues it is a distraction, or worse, from the job of keeping their charges under control.
When a prison warder checked on him at 13:00 GMT, Spiller said he was feeling better, after being allowed to make the phone call.
